event: Tolowa Dee-ni' establish seasonal villages at Smith River estuary
Cascadia subduction zone earthquake (M~9.0); tsunami strikes coast
Spanish expedition of Heceta and Quadra sails past coast
Smith party attacked at Umpqua River by Chetco/Tututni
Jedediah Smith reaches Pacific coast at Smith River mouth via overland route
Crescent City townsite laid out
Yontocket Massacre: Tolowa Nee-dash gathering attacked; source traditions record hundreds killed
Howonquet Massacre: 70+ Tolowa killed; village burned
Crescent City incorporated; United Methodist Church built
Achulet Massacre near Lake Earl; est. 65-150 Tolowa killed
Battery Point Massacre at Crescent City
Del Norte County formally carved from Klamath County
Federal census records approximately 2,500 residents
Camp Lincoln established 13 June 1862; 800 Tolowa held captive
Smith River Reservation established for Tolowa Dee-ni'
First large-scale steam sawmill begins
Smith River Reservation reduced in size
Northwestern Pacific Railroad grading begins
Dolbeer steam donkey patented (U.S. Patent 256,553)
County produces 30M+ board feet annually
Smith River Rancheria established (160 acres)
First harbor jetty completed
Save the Redwoods League founded (Merriam, Grant, Osborn)
Highway access improves as trunk-rail connection remains unbuilt
Prairie Creek and Del Norte Coast Redwoods State Parks established
Edward Sapir records Tolowa language and oral tradition
Hobbs, Wall & Company mill closes after CIO unionization
Pearl Harbor attack; WWII coastal defense
State of Jefferson inaugurated 4 Dec; Pearl Harbor 7 Dec ends movement
Jedediah Smith Redwoods State Park established
County-seat trading-area population series crests near 5,600-5,700 during peak lumber
California Rancheria Termination Act (P.L. 85-671) signed 18 August
Jerry Crew finds 16-inch footprints at Bluff Creek; Genzoli coins Bigfoot
Smith River Rancheria termination effective under 1958 Act
Alaska earthquake tsunami devastates Crescent City; 11 killed
Patterson-Gimlin Bigfoot film shot at Bluff Creek 20 October
Redwood National Park established
Smith River Reservation expanded under federal trust
Indian Self-Determination Act empowers tribal governance
Redwood National Park expanded to 110,000 acres
Redwood parks designated UNESCO World Heritage Site
Smith River designated Wild & Scenic; only major undammed CA river
Tillie Hardwick v. United States restores 17 California rancherias 19 July
Simpson Timber Company closes regional operations
Pelican Bay State Prison opens December (275 acres; 1300-1600 jobs)
Smith River National Recreation Area Act (305,337 acres) signed 16 November
Pacific Lumber Company regional closure
Last Del Norte sawmill closes; Redwood NSP joint management formalized
M/V Stuyvesant spills 2,100 gal IFO-180 fuel oil one mile offshore 6 Sept
Klamath River fish kill: 33-78k salmon die below Iron Gate Dam
Cascadia subduction zone paleoseismology confirms 1700 event
Crescent City designated first California TsunamiReady community
Tolowa Dee-ni' cultural landscapes study published
November Kuril Islands tsunami damages Crescent City harbor (16 boats sunk)
FEMA tsunami retrofit guide published
Tōhoku earthquake sends tsunami currents into Crescent City harbor; one death occurs at Klamath River mouth
NOAA updates tsunami hazard maps for Crescent City
USGS completes tsunami hazard model for harbor
Yurok Tribe begins dam removal advocacy on Klamath
City General Plan update incorporates sea-level rise projections
Smith River salmonid habitat restoration progress report
Census records 6,673 residents including Pelican Bay group quarters
COVID-19 pandemic; community mutual aid activates
Slater Fire burns 157,270 acres across Del Norte/Curry/Siskiyou
Del Norte County Long-Range Transportation Plan published
Tolowa Dee-ni' language revitalization program expands
Redwood N&SP resource management assessment completed
Smith River Complex wildfire burns 95,107 acres
Yurok-Tolowa Dee-ni-Cher-Ae Heights IMSA designated 22 September
Caltrans selects Alternative F, an approximately one-mile tunnel bypass, for Last Chance Grade
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if Caltrans changes the selected alternative, cost estimate, construction window, or preferred-alternative page.
label: Last Chance Grade tunnel selected

event: Iron Gate cofferdam breached 28 August; Klamath River reopens to anadromous fish before final project completion
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if KRRC, NOAA, or state agencies publish a correction to the August 28 free-flowing milestone, October 2 completion record, habitat-mile update, or post-removal status change.
label: Iron Gate cofferdam breached

event: Klamath Free-flowing River Ceremony, 2 October; KRRC Definite Plan completion
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if KRRC updates the completion record, ceremony status, or lower Klamath restoration milestone.
label: Klamath free-flowing ceremony

event: CDFW sets limited California ocean salmon recreational season after 2023-2024 closures
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if CDFW or federal managers revise the 2025 ocean salmon season, harvest guideline, or closure record.
label: Limited salmon opener

event: CDFW reports widespread Klamath salmon reoccupation one year after dam removal, while final adult-return estimates remain pending
refresh_trigger: Re-audit when CDFW, KRRC, NOAA, USGS, or tribal monitoring partners publish final adult-return estimates, revised tributary counts, or post-removal recovery updates.
label: Klamath salmon monitoring signal

event: CDFW announces commercial ocean salmon return after a three-year closure, with in-season management
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if CDFW, PFMC, or federal managers revise the 2026 commercial or recreational ocean salmon season.
label: Salmon commercial return

event: Del Norte Triplicate briefly closes after 146 years under Country Media, then is purchased for a local relaunch; civic press also shifts toward Redwood Voice and Wild Coast Compass
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if the Triplicate ownership, relaunch status, publication schedule, or local-media landscape changes.
label: Triplicate closure + relaunch

event: Pelican Bay State Prison homicide investigation opens (Gabriel Otero, 10 March)
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if CDCR, the district attorney, or court records update the homicide investigation or charging status.
label: Pelican Bay homicide investigation

event: Battery Point Apartments $100M affordable-housing project resumes construction after $9.7M HOME-funds award
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if the city, developer, funder, or local reporting changes construction, financing, or unit-count status.
label: Battery Point Apartments resumes

event: Crescent City Council opens Prop 218 protest process for proposed water and sewer rate increases; June 1 public hearing set
evidence_type: official agenda and local civic reporting
refresh_trigger: Re-audit after the June 1, 2026 hearing, protest count, ordinance vote, or any changed rate effective date.
label: Water/sewer Prop 218 process

event: Maiden Lane fatal altercation on 16 March; three men later accused in killing of 41-year-old Robert Allen Cole
refresh_trigger: Re-audit when sheriff, district attorney, or court records update charges, hearings, disposition, or victim details.
label: Maiden Lane homicide charges

event: Crescent City named America's Favorite Small Towns finalist by Parade Magazine and Steller (March)
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if Parade or Steller changes finalist status, contest results, ranking, or eligibility language.
label: Small-town finalist

event: Tolowa Dee-ni' Nation awarded $200,000 for elk-habitat restoration
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if Tolowa Dee-ni' Nation, USFWS, CDFW, or grant records update funding, acreage, or project scope.
label: Tolowa elk-habitat grant

event: Mw 4.8 offshore Gorda-plate earthquake 89 km WSW of Crescent City, 9 May; no tsunami flag
refresh_trigger: Re-audit if USGS revises magnitude, location, event ID, tsunami flag, or authoritative event-page metadata.
label: Mw 4.8 offshore quake

event: Crescent City Fire & Rescue opens fire chief recruitment (5 May); applications close 5 June
refresh_trigger: Re-audit after the June 5, 2026 application deadline or if the recruitment notice, schedule, or appointment changes.
label: Fire chief recruitment

event: Del Norte County primary election (2 June): contested Board of Supervisors, State Assembly, and Senate District 2 seats
evidence_type: scheduled election record and local reporting
refresh_trigger: Re-audit after June 2, 2026 or if county or state election offices change ballot, candidate, or result status.
label: Scheduled June primary
